mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS) creates uncertainty that depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 225 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic demand that supports corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises > 1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer demand that drives economic growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market that boosts consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates (FEDFUNDS) during a tightening cycle compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3pp month-over-month.
